channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Present and Accounted For: House Republicans' small majority could make attendance a priority
item.description: While Congress doesn't usually take attendance, the GOP's small majority may make figuring out who is present a dramatic daily event in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Popular gun manufacturer thanks Elon Musk after being suspended by Facebook
item.description: After its Facebook account was suspended, Smith & Wesson thanked Elon Musk and X for supporting free speech amid what it called ongoing attacks against the First and Second Amendments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Supreme Court will hear arguments over Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for minors
item.description: The Supreme Court is set to hear arguments on the constitutionality of Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for transgender minors.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman And Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Inside how podcasters moved the voting needle with young men ahead of Trump's massive win
item.description: President-elect Trump joined his first podcast in 2022 with the Nelk Boys, ahead of joining dozens of podcasts and earning support through an initiative called Send the Vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic lawmakers report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: At least five Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut were targeted by bomb threats on Thursday. Police who responded said they found no evidence of explosives on the lawmakers' properties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Massachusetts lawmakers push for an effort to ban all tobacco sales over time
item.description: A handful of Massachusetts lawmakers are hoping to persuade their colleagues to support a proposal that would make the state the first to adopt a ban meant to eliminate the use of tobacco products over time.
item.dc:creator: Steve Leblanc, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democratic lawmakers from Connecticut report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: Six Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut have been targeted by bomb threats on their homes.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The outlook is uncertain for AI regulations as the US government pivots to full Republican control
item.description: The federal government is about to transition from one that prioritized safeguards for artificial intelligence to one more focused on eliminating red tape.
item.dc:creator: Dan Merica And Ali Swenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: A spokesperson for Meta confirmed that Zuckerberg and Trump met on Wednesday, saying he was invited for dinner with the president-elect to talk about the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Top 5 biggest political turkeys ripped by conservatives in 2024
item.description: Prominent Democrats experienced a disappointing 2024 across the political spectrum after President-elect Trump returned to the White House after a contentious election cycle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump transition says Cabinet picks, appointees were targeted by bomb threats, swatting attacks
item.description: Among those targeted were New York Rep. Elise Stefanik, Trump's pick to serve as ambassador to the United Nations, and Matt Gaetz, Trump's initial pick to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Zuckerberg dines with Trump in Mar-a-Lago
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump dined on Wednesday with Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, bringing together the Facebook founder and the former president who was once banned from that social network.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Americans' opinion of Republican Party on the rise
item.description: In the poll, 45 percent of Americans said they feel “favorable” toward the Republican Party, up 6 points from an Economist/YouGov poll in late October, when 39 percent said they felt favorable toward GOP.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: No Labels pushes back after opposition to third-party run
item.description: No Labels, a centrist group, claims in court documents that some Democrats worked to oppose a third-party candidate
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) - Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota cannabis license lottery challenge moves to state appeals court
item.description: The move comes after a district court judge this week blocked a state lottery to choose which social equity applicants would be pre-licensed to start up cannabis businesses.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: End of Trump prosecutions renews scrutiny of DOJ's pace
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith's move to dismiss Donald Trump's two federal indictments has sparked finger-pointing from those eager to see the president-elect held to account, with critics airing their frustrations at the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the courts for the anticlimactic end to the case.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 4 takeaways from Trump's health agency nominations
item.description: Almost all have been highly critical of the agencies they would run if confirmed, and experts say the nominees represent a complete overhaul of the vision and priorities of key agencies for health care and public health.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico suggests it would impose its own tariffs to retaliate against any Trump tariffs
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — President Claudia Sheinbaum suggested Tuesday that Mexico could retaliate with tariffs of its own, after U.S. President-elect Donald Trump threatened to impose 25% import duties on Mexican goods if the country doesn’t stop the flow of drugs and migrants across the border.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Surveillance tech advances by Biden could aid in Trump's promised crackdown on immigration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump will return to power next year with a raft of technological tools at his disposal that would help deliver his campaign promise of cracking down on immigration — among them, surveillance and artificial intelligence technology that the Biden administration already uses to
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, GARANCE BURKE and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's hush money case in limbo after judge adjourns sentencing
item.description: New York Judge Juan Merchan faces a choice of freezing President-elect Trump's hush money case in place until he leaves the White House or tossing it entirely, underscoring how Trump's election win has changed the dynamic of his legal travails.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump rounds out his second Cabinet
item.description: President-elect Trump is rounding out his Cabinet just a few weeks after securing a second White House term, filling the top jobs that will carry out his agenda in the years to come.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens Canada, Mexico, China with tariffs
item.description: He said the countries weren't doing enough to control the flow of illegal drugs, especially fentanyl, and migrants across the border.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota House recounts keep two DFLers in the lead, court challenge likely
item.description: Officials in two counties revisited results in close House races on Monday after experiencing vote reporting issues after Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota DNR delays decision on key proposed copper-nickel mine permit
item.description: The Minnesota Department of Natural Resources has delayed a decision until late next year on a key permit for the proposed NewRange Copper Nickel project in northeastern Minnesota, the mine formerly known as PolyMet. The company called the agency’s decision “unfortunate.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Lawsuit challenges Minnesota abortion access in federal court
item.description: Minnesota has some of the most accommodating laws granting access to abortion. A lawsuit filed late last week seeks to upend them.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth, Anna Haecherl, and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Florida health official advises communities to stop adding fluoride to drinking water
item.description: Florida’s surgeon general, Dr. Joseph Ladapo, cited developmental concerns from higher levels of fluoride than are found in most U.S. water supplies.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Colombini, MPR News

item.title: The rising price of paying the national debt is a risk for Trump’s promises on growth and inflation
item.description: Donald Trump has big plans for the economy. He also has big debt problem that’ll be a hurdle to delivering on those plan. Trump has bold ambitions on tax cuts, tariffs and other programs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Here are the people Trump has picked for key positions so far
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is filling key posts in his second administration, and it's shaping up much differently than his first. He's prioritizing loyalists for top jobs.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, Fox 5
